attempts.error: SyntaxError: * argument may appear only once in RuntimeSupervisor.run_blocking signature.
attempts.diagnosis: The signature attempted to combine keyword-only marker with variadic args incorrectly.
attempts.fix_tried: Removed the keyword-only marker while preserving keyword-call compatibility for named parameters before `*args`.

attempts.error: Ruff UP035/F401 for Callable import and unused time in tests.
attempts.diagnosis: New module/test did not meet repo Ruff lint rules.
attempts.fix_tried: Moved Callable to collections.abc and removed unused test import.

attempts.error: Ruff TC003 requested Callable under TYPE_CHECKING.
attempts.diagnosis: With future annotations, Callable is type-only and repo enables TCH rules.
attempts.fix_tried: Moved Callable import into TYPE_CHECKING block and reran lint/tests.

wisdom.content: For Python 3.13/Ruff TCH rules with `from __future__ import annotations`, `collections.abc.Callable` used only in annotations must be imported inside `if TYPE_CHECKING:`. Ruff may report both UP035 and TC003 in sequence.

When adding an MCP tool in this repo, update both the server import/export surface and the explicit server tool registration/count test; otherwise focused tool-list tests fail even if the tool implementation is correct.

For status APIs that must become cheap by default while preserving compatibility, keep legacy required fields and add explicit optional fields like `summary_only`/`detail` so callers can distinguish omitted expensive counts from real zero counts.

proposal.approval_evidence: User approved proposal with Tier A reply: continue

discovery.approval_evidence: Discovery findings recorded, user approved acceptance criteria via `approve`, agreement persisted, and ChangeContract minted with 19 items.

design.approval_evidence: Design recorded. Independent validator returned VALIDATED; caution about no-arg deep mode incorporated by keeping no-arg status cheap-only and requiring scoped path for deep stats. No unresolved conflicts or contract-compromise risk.

planning.approval_evidence: User approved prep plan via inline reply `approve`. Task graph has 8 tasks with contract refs and validation passed; warning NO_DELTAS is expected because spec delta task owns new daemon spec.

Acceptance evidence: full pytest 597 passed, full ruff passed, spec JSON valid, independent reviewer READY with no findings, contract review matrix 19/19 pass/respected, clean worktree at b95ed0784ef763e268c4910245b9582978c121b0.

items.text: Timed-out/cancelled expensive daemon jobs are visible in job state as cancelled/abandoned/finished; never stuck “active” forever.
`lgrep_status_semantic(path="")` returns cheap bounded summary by default and avoids deep LanceDB per-project counts unless explicitly scoped/deep.
Diagnostics expose PID/uptime, loaded projects, active/recent jobs, job kind, full local project path, age, and terminal state; no secrets/env values.
Blocking sync work uses bounded runtime supervision, not unbounded default-executor fanout.
Shared cache/worktree safety remains intact; no silent chunk deletion/corruption and prune transport guards stay enforced.
Tests cover timeout/abandonment state, bounded global status, diagnostics shape, and conflict-marker rejection.
Docs explain Vision/shared HTTP safe settings and high-CPU/thread troubleshooting.
Preserve existing MCP tool names and response compatibility unless design explicitly justifies an additive or versioned change.
Prefer structural correctness: job registry, typed response contracts, bounded executors, deterministic tests, and explicit runtime state over log scraping or heuristic inference.
Keep shared-cache safety invariants from `lgrepSemanticCacheLifecycle`; do not delete or mutate live cache data implicitly.
Do not expose secrets, environment values, or API keys in diagnostics.
Do not weaken prune transport-safety guards.
Avoid product novelty before daemon stability; graph/local-embedding ideas remain out of scope.
Do not make correctness depend on heuristic process-name/thread-count guessing alone.
Do not let global status trigger unbounded per-project disk/LanceDB work by default.
Do not silently discard or corrupt shared worktree cache data while improving cleanup/visibility.
Do not restart or destructively clean the live Vision-managed daemon/cache as part of implementation without explicit operator action.
Do not broaden into graph/code-impact features, local embedding backend migration, or public internet deployment platform work.

rows.evidence: tests/test_daemon_runtime.py covers timeout/cancellation abandonment and eventual terminal states; full pytest passed 597 tests.
rows.notes: RuntimeSupervisor records abandoned/finished_after_abandon/failed_after_abandon rather than leaving active jobs stuck.

rows.evidence: tests/test_server.py bounded global status test asserts no-arg status does not call DB count methods and returns summary_only entries; full pytest passed.
rows.notes: Scoped path status still performs deep counts through runtime supervision.

rows.evidence: tests/test_diagnostics.py covers diagnostics shape, loaded project paths, active/recent jobs, abandonment summary, and secret/env exclusion; full pytest passed.
tests/test_server.py, tests/test_watcher.py, tests/test_daemon_runtime.py verify semantic/index/status/watcher/maintenance blocking work routes through RuntimeSupervisor; full pytest and ruff passed.
rows.notes: Remaining asyncio.to_thread uses are fallback or symbol-tool paths outside this daemon-safety task scope.

rows.evidence: tests/test_worktree_cache.py, tests/test_prune_orphans.py, and MCP prune transport tests passed in full pytest; destructive shared HTTP prune remains forced dry-run.
rows.notes: No live daemon/cache cleanup was performed.

rows.evidence: Full pytest passed 597 tests including tests/test_daemon_runtime.py, bounded global status tests, tests/test_diagnostics.py, and tests/test_conflict_markers.py.
README.md, skills/lgrep/SKILL.md, and src/lgrep/install_opencode.py updated; docs-focused tests passed.
rows.notes: Docs cover Vision/shared HTTP, warm paths, auto-warm, worktree dedup, timeouts, worker limit, diagnostics, and troubleshooting.

rows.evidence: CHANGELOG.md conflict markers removed under tk-83b6a4a2bfd0; tests/test_conflict_markers.py passed in full pytest.
rows.notes: Full pytest passed with conflict marker guard.

rows.evidence: Existing tool names preserved; lgrep_diagnostics added as additive MCP tool. Status response kept legacy fields and added optional summary_only/detail fields.
RuntimeSupervisor, JobStatus enum, TypedDict response contracts, contract tests, and deterministic pytest coverage own correctness.
rows.notes: No log scraping or CPU/thread heuristics used for correctness.

rows.evidence: Worktree cache and prune suites passed; invalidation and prune safety code routes through runtime without changing deletion invariants.
rows.notes: No implicit live cache mutation added.

rows.evidence: tests/test_diagnostics.py asserts diagnostics exclude VOYAGE_API_KEY and env values; full pytest passed.
rows.notes: Diagnostics include worker_max_threads but not raw env variable names/values.

rows.evidence: Shared HTTP prune dry-run test passed; full prune/worktree suites passed.
rows.notes: Shared HTTP destructive prune remains refused/coerced to dry run.

Reviewer verdict READY; diagnostics are explicit RuntimeSupervisor state, not heuristic process-name/thread-count inference.
No-arg status test asserts DB count/get_indexed_files are not called.
Worktree cache/prune suites passed; no silent cache deletion behavior added.
All work ran in isolated change worktree; no live Vision-managed daemon restart or destructive live cache cleanup was performed.
Changed surface remains daemon safety and operator guidance only.
rows.notes: No graph/code-impact features or embedding backend migration added.

acceptanceCriteria: Timed-out/cancelled expensive daemon jobs are visible in job state as cancelled/abandoned/finished; never stuck “active” forever.
`lgrep_status_semantic(path="")` returns cheap bounded summary by default and avoids deep LanceDB per-project counts unless explicitly scoped/deep.
Diagnostics expose PID/uptime, loaded projects, active/recent jobs, job kind, full local project path, age, and terminal state; no secrets/env values.
Blocking sync work uses bounded runtime supervision, not unbounded default-executor fanout.
Shared cache/worktree safety remains intact; no silent chunk deletion/corruption and prune transport guards stay enforced.
Tests cover timeout/abandonment state, bounded global status, diagnostics shape, and conflict-marker rejection.
Docs explain Vision/shared HTTP safe settings and high-CPU/thread troubleshooting.

clarify_findings.message: Success criteria are placeholder or empty. What measurable outcomes define done?
